description_long: This object is a long, slender sword with a distinctive wavy blade, reminiscent of a kris or flamberge style. The blade is undulating along its entire length, giving it a serpentine appearance, and appears to be made of metal with a dark, matte finish. The handle is short, slightly curved, and solid, with no visible wrapping or elaborate grip, and it is visually distinct from the blade by its darker coloration. There is no visible crossguard or hand protection between the blade and handle, making the transition between the two smooth and minimalistic. The sword lacks any visible ornamentation or decorative elements, emphasizing a utilitarian design. The overall proportions suggest it is designed for one-handed use, and its unique blade shape would make it stand out in any urban or historical context.
description: A long, wavy-bladed sword with a simple, curved handle and no visible ornamentation or guard. The blade is dark and serpentine, giving it a striking, utilitarian appearance.
description_view_0: The wavy blade and curved handle are clearly visible, showing the full serpentine profile of the sword.
description_view_1: The sword is seen edge-on, making the blade appear as a thin, straight line with the handle as a small rounded end.
description_view_2: The opposite side of the wavy blade is visible, mirroring the profile seen in view 0, with the handle's curve apparent.
description_view_3: Another edge-on view, similar to view 1, with the blade appearing as a thin line and the handle as a rounded end.
